did_this_paper_use_ai_to_obtain_results_.details: The paper proposes CQC-Net, a lightweight deep learning model utilizing consistent orientational quaternion wavelet convolution for skin lesion segmentation. It uses quaternion neural networks (QNNs), specifically an orientation-consistent quaternion convolution (OCQC) module and a quaternion enhanced feedforward network (QEFN), to segment melanoma lesions in the ISIC 2016, ISIC 2017, and ISIC 2018 datasets.

is_this_paper_a_review_paper_or_a_resear.details: The paper presents original experimental work proposing a new deep learning model, CQC-Net, for skin lesion segmentation. It details the methodology (OCQC module, QEFN, QWT), implementation details (datasets, hyperparameters), and extensive experimental results (IoU scores, FLOPs, parameters) comparing the proposed method against state-of-the-art approaches on ISIC 2016, 2017, and 2018 datasets.

title: CQC-Net: A Lightweight Model Utilizing Consistent Orientational Quaternion Wavelet Convolution for Skin Lesion Segmentation
brief_summary: This study proposes CQC-Net, a lightweight deep learning model for skin lesion segmentation that utilizes consistent orientational quaternion wavelet convolution to balance performance and computational efficiency. The architecture integrates an orientation-consistent quaternion convolution (OCQC) module and a quaternion enhanced feedforward network (QEFN) within the quaternion wavelet domain to capture directional features and enhance multi-frequency interactions. The model was evaluated on the ISIC 2016, ISIC 2017, and ISIC 2018 datasets using metrics such as intersection over union (IoU), dice similarity coefficient (DSC), accuracy, specificity, and sensitivity. Experimental results demonstrated that CQC-Net achieved state-of-the-art performance with IoU scores of 86.15%, 80.78%, and 82.09% on the respective datasets. Furthermore, the model maintained high efficiency with only 0.86 million parameters and 2.96 G FLOPs, significantly outperforming larger models in terms of computational cost while delivering superior segmentation accuracy.

lesion_types_studied.details: The paper focuses on skin lesion segmentation for automated melanoma diagnosis using the ISIC 2016, ISIC 2017, and ISIC 2018 datasets. The task is binary segmentation of lesion regions (melanoma/other skin lesions) from background, rather than multi-class histopathological classification.

all_task_types.target: Skin lesion masks (binary segmentation of melanoma lesions from background)
all_task_types.details: The paper proposes CQC-Net for skin lesion segmentation. The model predicts pixel-wise masks for lesion regions. Performance is evaluated using segmentation metrics such as Intersection over Union (IoU), Dice Similarity Coefficient (DSC), Accuracy, Specificity, and Sensitivity on the ISIC 2016, 2017, and 2018 datasets.

datasets_used.name: ISIC 2016
datasets_used.description: A skin lesion dataset curated by the International Skin Imaging Collaboration (ISIC) to support automated melanoma diagnosis, containing dermoscopic images.

use.training: Used for training the CQC-Net model.
use.testing: Used for testing and performance evaluation (IoU, DSC, etc.).

datasets_used.name: ISIC 2017
datasets_used.description: A skin lesion dataset curated by the International Skin Imaging Collaboration (ISIC) to support automated melanoma diagnosis, containing dermoscopic images.

use.training: Used for training the CQC-Net model (70% split).
use.validation: Used for validation during training (30% split).
use.testing: Used for testing and performance evaluation (IoU, DSC, etc.).

datasets_used.name: ISIC 2018
datasets_used.description: A skin lesion dataset curated by the International Skin Imaging Collaboration (ISIC) to support automated melanoma diagnosis, containing dermoscopic images.

use.training: Used for training the CQC-Net model (70% split).
use.validation: Used for validation during training (30% split).
use.testing: Used for testing and performance evaluation (IoU, DSC, etc.).

hypothesis_testing.interpretation: The paper did not report any formal hypothesis testing, p-values, or significance tests. Performance comparisons were made using point estimates of metrics (IoU, DSC, etc.) without statistical significance analysis.

ISIC 2016.notes: CQC-Net achieved state-of-the-art performance, surpassing UNetV2 in mIoU, DSC, Acc, and sensitivity. Visual comparisons showed CQC-Net avoided over-segmentation and holes present in other methods.

ISIC 2017.notes: CQC-Net outperformed VM-UNetV2 with improvements of 0.50% in mIoU, 0.31% in DSC, 0.22% in Acc, and 0.34% in Spe. It effectively handled low color contrast lesion edges.

ISIC 2018.notes: CQC-Net showed a clear advantage over VM-UNet, improving mIoU by 1.04%, DSC by 0.64%, Acc by 0.28%, and Sen by 0.26%. It effectively suppressed hair interference and segmented visually ambiguous areas.

Ablation Study (ISIC 2017).notes: Setting (a) decreased mIoU by 1.95% and DSC by 1.21% compared to baseline due to loss of directional consistency. Setting (b) caused further decline due to redundant information. Setting (c) showed performance drop due to limitations of QPConv in modeling spatial dependencies.

Ablation Study (ISIC 2018).notes: Uncertainty modeling allowed adaptive learning of loss weights, resulting in superior performance compared to fixed weight combinations like Dice + BCE.

Efficiency Comparison.notes: CQC-Net has the lowest number of parameters (0.86 M) and requires significantly less memory (3.40 MB) than most competing approaches, making it suitable for real-world medical deployment.

architecture_details.backbone: U-Net-like encoder-decoder architecture with quaternion wavelet transforms

ocqc_details.function: Extracts direction-specific features from quaternion wavelet sub-bands (LL, LH, HL, HH)

qefn_details.qefn_structure: Second QDSC projects channel dimensions to twice original size; IQDSC restores to initial dimensions

architecture_details.residual_connections: Preserve original sub-band features and directional information across stages

training_details.learning_rate: Initial 1e-3, decayed via CosineAnnealingLR to 1e-5 over 50 iterations
training_details.loss_function: Total loss combining Dice loss, Binary Cross-Entropy (BCE) loss, and Focal loss with uncertainty modeling (learnable weights lambda1, lambda2, lambda3 initialized to 1)

classification_or_output_head.details: Final output generated via inverse QWT reconstruction

preprocessing_techniques: Resizing images to 256 × 256 pixels before inputting into the model
Data augmentation: vertical and horizontal flips with a probability of 0.5
Data augmentation: random rotations between 0° and 360° with a probability of 0.5

data_augmentation_techniques.details: Applied between 0° and 360° with a probability of 0.5.

conclusions: The proposed CQC-Net model achieves state-of-the-art performance in skin lesion segmentation on the ISIC 2016, ISIC 2017, and ISIC 2018 datasets, with IoU scores of 86.15%, 80.78%, and 82.09%, respectively.

CQC-Net effectively addresses limitations in prior methods by utilizing orientation-consistent quaternion convolution (OCQC) to capture distinct directional features (vertical, horizontal, diagonal) and avoid redundant feature learning.
The quaternion enhanced feedforward network (QEFN) successfully facilitates interaction among frequency bands, improving boundary perception and handling of low color contrast lesion edges.

The model is highly lightweight, requiring only 0.86 million parameters and 2.96 G FLOPs, which is significantly lower than competing state-of-the-art models like VM-UNetV2 (22.77 M parameters, 4.40 G FLOPs).

CQC-Net demonstrates superior computational efficiency, requiring only 3.40 MB of memory compared to tens to hundreds of times more for other approaches, making it suitable for real-world medical deployment.
The integration of the quaternion wavelet transform (QWT) improves generalization by providing translation invariance and handling lesions with varying shapes and positions.

Ablation studies confirm that the specific design of the OCQC module (directional consistency) and QEFN (multi-frequency interaction) are critical for performance, as rearranging branches or using square convolutions leads to performance degradation.
Uncertainty modeling in the loss function allows for adaptive learning of loss weights, resulting in superior segmentation accuracy compared to fixed weight combinations like Dice + BCE.
CQC-Net effectively suppresses hair interference and segments visually ambiguous areas where other methods often fail or produce over-segmentation/holes.
important_remarks: The study relies exclusively on retrospective, publicly available ISIC datasets (2016, 2017, 2018) without external validation on independent clinical cohorts, limiting generalizability to diverse real-world populations.
No statistical significance testing (e.g., p-values) was performed; performance claims are based solely on point estimates of metrics like IoU and DSC.

The model was trained on a single GPU (RTX 4070 Super) with a fixed 7:3 train-validation split for ISIC 2017/2018, raising potential concerns about data leakage or overfitting to specific dataset splits.

While the model is lightweight (0.86M parameters), it was not tested for inference speed or latency on edge devices or mobile hardware, which is critical for the claimed 'real-world medical deployment' suitability.

The paper does not report performance on other common skin lesion datasets (e.g., PH2, Derm7pt) or handle multi-class classification (e.g., distinguishing melanoma from benign nevi), restricting the scope to binary segmentation only.
